compiled_state.title: Integrate Semgrep with mandatory security rulesets into CI pipeline template
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context

Teams using DSO's /dso:onboarding to scaffold CI pipelines currently get Bandit for security scanning — a Python-only tool that leaves JavaScript, Go, Terraform, and other stacks uncovered. Government teams need --metrics=off to prevent telemetry transmission, and there's no unified SAST gate across language stacks. Semgrep OSS covers 30+ languages in a single ~10-second scan, making it a direct replacement for Bandit with broader coverage at comparable speed.

## Success Criteria

- The CI pipeline template (examples/ci.example.yml) runs Semgrep OSS instead of Bandit, using a pinned semgrep/semgrep Docker container tag with --metrics=off and --error; SARIF output is written to semgrep-results.sarif and uploaded as a GitHub Actions artifact. SARIF upload to GitHub Security tab (via upload-sarif with continue-on-error: true) includes a jq post-processing step to convert string severity labels to CVSS numerics (workaround for semgrep/semgrep#10834). SARIF upload is best-effort and decoupled from the blocking CI gate.
- A generate-semgrep-config.sh script produces a .semgrep.yml with mandatory rulesets (p/security-audit, p/owasp-top-ten, p/cwe-top-25) plus stack-specific rulesets mapped from detect-stack.sh output: python-poetry -> p/python; node-npm -> p/javascript; golang -> p/golang; convention-based/unknown -> mandatory rulesets only. The generated config passes semgrep --validate.
- /dso:onboarding calls generate-semgrep-config.sh during CI setup and writes these config keys to dso-config.conf: semgrep.enabled (bool, default true), semgrep.metrics (string, default "off"), semgrep.fail_on (string: error|warning, default "error"), semgrep.custom_rulesets (comma-separated paths, default empty).
- The CI step runs semgrep scan --error --severity ERROR (or --severity WARNING --severity ERROR when semgrep.fail_on=warning), causing exit code 1 when findings exist at or above the configured threshold. The PR is blocked via required status check. The blocking gate depends on Semgrep's exit code, not on SARIF upload success.
- INSTALL.md and README each contain a Semgrep section covering: Docker prerequisite, the --metrics=off flag and its purpose for government teams, how to add custom rulesets via semgrep.custom_rulesets, and how to change the fail_on threshold.

## Scenario Analysis

- SARIF output not written on non-zero exit (high, runtime): CI upload step must use if: always() to run regardless of scan exit code
- Large monorepo OOM (critical, runtime): Semgrep can exhaust memory on 10k+ file repos; container exits with ambiguous code
- Stale stack detection (high, runtime): .semgrep.yml generated at onboarding time does not update when project adds new language stacks
- Registry network fetch failure (critical, runtime): p/ rulesets require network; partial fetch produces incomplete coverage
- Bandit migration overlap (high, deployment): Existing projects retain Bandit config after re-onboarding
- Worktree CWD mismatch (high, deployment): generate-semgrep-config.sh must use REPO_ROOT, not relative paths
- Unpinned Docker image (medium, deployment): Mutable tags cause silent behavior changes
- SARIF format drift (high, deployment): Unpinned Semgrep versions may emit incompatible SARIF
- Warning-severity bypass (critical, configuration): configurable via semgrep.fail_on (default error; set to warning for stricter enforcement)
- Custom ruleset YAML breakage (high, configuration): Invalid semgrep.custom_rulesets can produce unparseable .semgrep.yml
- Metrics env var override (critical, configuration): SEMGREP_SEND_METRICS env var can override --metrics=off flag
- Stale config keys (medium, configuration): Re-onboarding does not specify overwrite vs merge behavior for semgrep.* keys

## Dependencies

- detect-stack.sh (exists in plugins/dso/scripts/; consumed as-is)
- /dso:onboarding CI setup phase (must accept injected config generation scripts)

## Differentiation from related epics

- 857d-a35a (shift-left security in planning): owns planning-time security declarations; this epic owns CI-time scanning. No shared artifacts.
- c821-e995 (security remediation recipes): consumes structured findings; this epic produces SARIF. Schema coordination out of scope for v1.
- dso-0wi2 (project config flags): semgrep.* keys follow existing dso-config.conf KEY=VALUE convention; no schema conflicts.

## Approach

Replace Bandit with Semgrep in the CI template using a config-driven design. A new generate-semgrep-config.sh script reads detect-stack.sh output to select language-specific rulesets alongside mandatory security rulesets, writing a .semgrep.yml for the project. Onboarding orchestrates this during CI setup. New semgrep.* keys in dso-config.conf control enablement, metrics, severity threshold, and custom rulesets.

## Research Findings

### External integration: Semgrep CLI and Docker
- Trigger: External integration
- Query: Semgrep OSS Docker container availability, --metrics=off, SARIF output, exit codes
- Source URLs: semgrep.dev/docs/cli-reference, hub.docker.com/r/semgrep/semgrep, semgrep.dev/docs/semgrep-ci/sample-ci-configs
- Key insight: semgrep/semgrep-action is deprecated (archived April 2024); use semgrep/semgrep Docker container directly. semgrep scan exits 0 by default even with findings; --error flag required for CI gate. --severity filters which findings are reported.

### External integration: SARIF compatibility
- Trigger: External integration
- Query: Semgrep SARIF upload to GitHub Security tab compatibility
- Source URLs: github.com/semgrep/semgrep/issues/10834, docs.github.com/en/code-security/code-scanning/integrating-with-code-scanning/uploading-a-sarif-file-to-github
- Key insight: Semgrep emits string severity labels ("Medium") but GitHub upload-sarif requires CVSS numerics. Open bug semgrep/semgrep#10834. Workaround: jq post-processing step to convert strings to numerics.

### External integration: Registry rulesets
- Trigger: Unfamiliar dependency
- Query: Semgrep Community Edition ruleset availability (p/security-audit, p/owasp-top-ten, p/cwe-top-25)
- Source URLs: semgrep.dev/p/owasp-top-ten, semgrep.dev/p/cwe-top-25, semgrep.dev/products/community-edition
- Key insight: All three mandatory rulesets confirmed available in Community Edition without login. 2800+ community rules accessible without authentication.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): triggered (user-requested)
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: External integration (Semgrep CLI/Docker/Registry), Unfamiliar dependency (Semgrep OSS)
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 12
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
